memory:memory/2026-04-03.md:37:58.snippet: - **Next step:** Try int4/int8 quantization for qualifier + target, OR use MLX backend (more memory efficient on Apple Silicon) ### MLX Breakthrough (05:20-06:16 EDT) - Switched to MLX backend — all 3 models load at **5.56 GB** peak (vs OOM with PyTorch) - Models: Qwen2.5-0.5B/1.5B/7B-Instruct-4bit via mlx-community - 2-model spec dec works via mlx-lm built-in: 67.6 tok/s (3B), 25 tok/s (7B) - 3-model PyramidSD loop runs but **KV cache desync bug** — rejected draft tokens advance the draft cache but not qualifier/target caches - Result: 2.4% draft acceptance, garbage output - Fix needed: proper cache trim/rollback on rejection - Pushed to GitHub: `e33467f` on v2-token-level branch - README

memory:memory/archive/2026-03-20.md:205:.snippet: - Workaround: Can force push with `--force` flag or disable rulesets on account **Resolution for future:** - Check account-level rulesets in GitHub settings - Either disable or adjust to allow standard pushes - Retry momotaro-ios push ### Lessons Learned - **Token scopes matter:** Initial token failed on GitHub Actions workflow files; `workflow` scope was needed - **Repo creation first:** Must create repos on target account before pushing (mirror push fails on non-existent repos) - **Rulesets are account-level:** Not just branch protection; newer GitHub feature can block pushes organization-wide - **Local config crucial:** Must update all local clones to point to new account before next pu

memory:memory/archive/2026-04-05.md:119:.snippet: "allow": ["openclaw-channel-rocketchat", "acpx", "telegram"], "entries": { "telegram": { "enabled": true } } } } ``` **Key Lesson:** After OpenClaw updates, if a channel silently stops working, check if it was moved from built-in to plugin. The symptom is zero log lines for that channel on startup — not even an error. **Detection Time:** ~20 minutes of debugging. The gateway was updated at 18:54 via `update.run`, and telegram worked under the old binary but failed on every restart after. --- ## Rocket.Chat fix April 2nd - Successfully debugged and resolved the Rocket.Chat channel issues. - Identified and fixed several root causes including conflicting legacy scripts

memory:memory/archive/2026-04-07.md:24:4.snippet: ``` - **Root cause chain:** 1. The `claude-cli/` provider prefix is no longer recognized in 2026.4.2 ("Missing auth: claude-cli" in gateway logs). 2. Gateway falls back to `google/gemini-2.5-pro`. 3. Gemini 2.5 Pro requires thinking mode, but the request had `budget: 0` (thinking off), causing the 400. - **Fix:** Updated the model config to use a properly-prefixed model (`anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6` direct, not via `claude-cli/`). - **Lesson:** After any gateway update, audit configured model identifiers for unrecognized provider prefixes. ### Regression 3: Rocket.Chat plugin SDK breakage (Apr 6 → Apr 7) - **Symptom:** RC channel in retry loop for 13+ hours overnight.

memory:memory/archive/2026-04-07.md:1:30.snippet: # Tuesday, April 7, 2026 ## OpenClaw 2026.4.2 Update — Multiple Regressions Discovered The auto-update on April 5 (~18:54 EDT) to gateway version **2026.4.2** introduced several silent regressions. Logging here as a consolidated reference because they keep biting us. ### Regression 1: Telegram channel no longer auto-loads (Apr 5, ~20:00 EDT) - **Symptom:** Telegram channel completely missing from gateway startup sequence after restart. No `[telegram]` log lines at all. Bot config in `openclaw.json` was correct and unchanged but channel never initialized. - **Root cause:** 2026.4.2 changed Telegram from a built-in channel to a plugin that requires explicit registration in `plugins.al
